report_name: Anthropic to Brief Global Financial Watchdog on 'Mythos' AI's Ability to Find Novel Cyber Flaws

attack_patterns.name: Vulnerability Scanning
attack_patterns.description: The 'Mythos' AI model demonstrated an unprecedented ability to autonomously discover novel high-severity software vulnerabilities.

tools.name: Mythos
tools.description: A powerful AI model developed by Anthropic that can autonomously discover novel software vulnerabilities, presenting a dual-use risk that could be weaponized for cyberattacks.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: AI safety and research company that developed the Mythos model and is set to brief the Financial Stability Board on its capabilities and risks.

identities.name: Financial Stability Board
identities.description: An international body responsible for monitoring the global financial system, which requested a briefing from Anthropic on the systemic risks posed by the Mythos AI model.

identities.name: Bank of England
identities.description: The UK's central bank, whose governor chairs the Financial Stability Board and requested the briefing on the Mythos AI model.

identities.name: Andrew Bailey
identities.description: Governor of the Bank of England and chair of the Financial Stability Board who expressed concern about the Mythos AI's potential to be weaponized.

identities.name: JPMorgan Chase
identities.description: One of approximately 40 organizations given limited access to the Mythos AI model for defensive purposes to patch flaws.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: One of approximately 40 organizations given limited access to the Mythos AI model for defensive purposes to patch flaws.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: One of approximately 40 organizations given limited access to the Mythos AI model for defensive purposes to patch flaws.

identities.name: UK AI Safety Institute
identities.description: A UK-based institute involved in evaluating the capabilities of the Mythos AI model.
